LAW AND MOTION 301, NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT VINCENT LIMA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN EXCUSE AND DILIGENCE. HE SHALL FILE HIS PROPOSED ANSWER PROMPTLY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR COSTS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, And Proposed Response
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael McCloskey (771.6174) appearing for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT VINCENT LIMA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant has shown excuse and diligence. He shall file his proposed answer promptly. Plaintiff's request for costs is denied. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189
